articles.title: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran

articles.title: US not 'turning back' on Asia allies, but expects them to boost defence - Hegseth
articles.description: The US defense secretary is questioned about US commitment, including further arms deals, at an Asian security summit in Singapore.

articles.title: Nato and EU condemn Russia after drone hits Romanian residential block
articles.description: Romania says the Russian drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defences and altered its trajectory.

articles.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave after 10 days

articles.summary: ガーナ議会が反LGBTQ+法案を可決。同性愛者、レズビアン、トランスジェンダーを自認する人々を対象とし、同性間の行為は禁固刑の対象となる。

articles.title: Spain's Sánchez digs in after eight years as PM as wave of scandals threatens survival

articles.description: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.

articles.titleJa: マルタで議会総選挙が行われる

articles.title: Germany news: Chaos possible as demo blocks Alpine pass
articles.description: A demonstration is planned at a major pass through the Alps, with huge traffic disruption possible. And a German minister is looking to crack down on screen time for kids. DW has more.

articles.title: Top US and Cuban generals meet at Guantanamo Bay

articles.summary: 米国とキューバの関係悪化の中、米国の高官がグアンタナモ湾でキューバ軍高官と異例の会談を行った。

articles.title: WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC
articles.description:
Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable disease
The death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.
Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: WHOがエボラ出血熱のアウトブレイクによる死亡率を「莫大な」30-50%と発表、事務局長がDRCに到着
articles.summary: 世界保健機関（WHO）は、コンゴ民主共和国（DRC）で発生しているエボラ出血熱の死亡率が30～50％と「莫大」であると発表。WHO事務局長がDRCに到着し、封じ込め努力を支援する。

articles.title: Friday briefing: ​What do the cuts in aid mean for the fight against Ebola in the DRC?
articles.description:
In today’s newsletter: As the virus spreads across borders, health workers warn that weakened global support is making a prolonged crisis more likely

Ebola is spreading rapidly in parts of east Africa. The deadly disease, which kills around half of those it infects, is suspected to have claimed the lives of at least 240 people since the outbreak began in Ituri province in the Democratic Republic of the Congo earlier this month.
Public health officials are scrambling to contain the virus in one of the toughest environments: Ituri province, the centre of the crisis, is a mining hub where thousands of people work in close proximity every day, and a conflict zone, with ongoing fighting between rebel groups. Medical facilities are modest, while waves of displaced people are being forced into overcrowded camps to escape fighting, making it even harder to control transmission. The virus has already spread to other regions in eastern DRC and the Ugandan capital Kampala.

articles.titleJa: 金曜日のブリーフィング：DRCのエボラ対策への援助削減は何を意味するのか？

articles.title: Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls’ school kills at least 16 students
articles.description:
Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county
A fire has ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya’s Rift valley, killing at least 16 students.
The fire broke out just after midnight at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-west of Nairobi, police said.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ケニアの女子校寮火災で少なくとも生徒16人が死亡
articles.summary: ケニアのナクル郡ギルギルにあるウツミシ女子アカデミーの寮で火災が発生し、少なくとも生徒16人が死亡した。真夜中の発生で、保護者は不安な状況が続いている。

articles.title: US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak
articles.description:
Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts
The Trump administration is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the Ebola outbreak, instead of bringing them home.
The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 米国、エボラ出血熱流行を受けケニアに米国人向け隔離センターを建設中

articles.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
articles.description:
Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC  
The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread.
Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict with the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province outpacing the response”. Tedros said on Monday that he would travel to the DRC this week.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: WHO事務局長、エボラ出血熱対策のためDRCでの停戦を要求
articles.summary: 世界保健機関（WHO）事務局長は、コンゴ民主共和国東部でエボラ出血熱が拡大していることを受け、紛争地域での即時停戦を要求した。ウガンダは国境を閉鎖し、事態の深刻化を警告している。

articles.title: Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence
articles.description:
Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflict
Mateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine El Confidente.
On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the long-running conflict between the army, paramilitaries and dissidents of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: コロンビア、政治的暴力の再燃が影を落とす中で選挙へ
articles.summary: コロンビアは、政治的暴力の再燃が影を落とす中で大統領選挙の投票準備を進めている。左右両派の候補者間での争いと、長年にわたる武装紛争への対処法が主な論点となっている。

articles.title: Alarm at Mexico bill allowing elections to be annulled for ‘foreign interference’
articles.description:
Opposition says constitutional amendment would give bill ruling party carte blanche to overturn will of voters

Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email

Amid fierce criticism from opposition groups, Mexico’s senate has passed ‌a constitutional amendment to include “foreign interference” as grounds to annul election results in the country.
The bill, which was presented by the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, defines foreign interference as “illicit financing, propaganda, the systematic ⁠dissemination of misinformation, digital manipulation, and ⁠the intervention of foreign governments ⁠or agencies”.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: メキシコ、「外国干渉」による選挙無効化法案に懸念広がる
articles.summary: メキシコ上院が「外国干渉」を理由に選挙結果を無効化できる憲法改正案を可決。野党は、与党が有権者の意思を覆す全権を得ると批判し、懸念が広がっている。

articles.title: Lula says Brazil will not be treated like ‘tinpot country’ after US designates gangs as terrorists
articles.description:
Marco Rubio made announcement after meeting president’s far-right challenger Flávio Bolsonaro
Brazil will not be treated as a “tinpot country,” the country’s president, Luiz Inácio da Silva, said on Friday after the United States designated Brazil’s two largest criminal gangs, the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command, as foreign terrorist organisations.
The announcement, made by Marco Rubio, the secretary of state, on Thursday, is being widely seen in Brazil as a setback for Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, the president who had strongly opposed the designation – and a boost for Lula’s main challenger in October’s presidential election, the far-right senator Flávio Bolsonaro.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ルーラ大統領、米国のギャングテロリスト指定を受け「ブラジルを小国のように扱わない」と発言
articles.summary: ブラジルのルーラ大統領は、米国がブラジルの主要ギャングをテロ組織に指定したことに対し、「ブラジルを小国のように扱ってはならない」と反発。この指定はルーラ氏にとって逆風となり、対立候補のボルソナーロ氏に有利と見られている。

articles.title: Canadian man admits sending ‘suicide packets’ to hundreds of people around world
articles.description:
Kenneth Law, who sold lethal chemicals online with instructions on how to use them, admits counselling or aiding suicide

 ‘It was too easy’: families ask how Kenneth Law enabled so many suicides

A Canadian man who mailed “suicide packets” of poison to more than 100 people in dozens of countries – including Canada, the UK, the US, Italy, Australia and New Zealand – has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of assisting suicide.
Kenneth Law appeared in a packed courtroom in Newmarket, Ontario, on Friday to enter the plea after prosecutors agreed to withdraw 14 murder charges. Sentencing is expected to take place in September.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: カナダ人男性、世界中の数百人に「自殺キット」を送付した罪を認める
articles.summary: カナダ人男性ケネス・ローが、世界中の100人以上に「自殺キット」を郵送し、自殺幇助の罪を認め14件の有罪判決を受けた。起訴された殺人罪14件は取り下げられた。

articles.title: Anger at decision not to prosecute Canadian suicide kit supplier in UK
articles.description:
Kenneth Law pleaded guilty in Canada to sending products internationally, knowing they would probably be used to end lives
Bereaved families whose loved ones were the victims of an online supplier of suicide kits say they feel insulted by a decision not to prosecute him in the UK.
Kenneth Law pleaded guilty in a court in Ontario, Canada, to 14 charges of aiding suicide and sending products internationally in the knowledge that they were likely to be used to end lives. He is due to be sentenced at a later date.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 英国でカナダ人自殺キット供給者を起訴しない決定に怒り
articles.summary: 自殺キット供給者ケネス・ローがカナダで有罪を認めたにもかかわらず、英国で起訴されない決定に対し、犠牲者の遺族から怒りの声が上がっている。英国での司法の判断が問われている。

articles.title: Tonight, we’re staying in: Patrick Bruel cancels his summer shows

articles.titleJa: 今夜はステイホーム：パトリック・ブリュエルが夏の公演を中止

articles.title: Sweden contributes SEK 2 million to WTO capacity-building activities
articles.description: Sweden has given SEK 2 million (just under CHF 170,000) to the WTO's Global Trust Fund for 2026 to finance technical assistance and training programmes for government officials from developing economies and least-developed countries (LDCs). The contribution was confirmed at a signing ceremony on 8 May at the WTO attended by Ambassador Nina Tornberg of Sweden and the WTO's Director of the Finance and Corporate Services Division, Andrew Rizk.
articles.titleJa: スウェーデン、WTOの能力開発活動に200万スウェーデンクローナを拠出
articles.summary: スウェーデンがWTOのグローバル信託基金に200万SEKを拠出し、途上国・後発開発途上国の政府職員向け技術支援を支援する。

articles.title: General Council chair outlines next steps to build on momentum from MC14 negotiations
articles.description: At the first meeting of the General Council since the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14), the newly elected Chairperson, Ambassador Clare Kelly (New Zealand), reported on her consultations with members on how to continue discussions in Geneva to build upon the high level of convergence on several areas of work at MC14 held in Yaoundé, Cameroon. Members also took note of Türkiye's announcement that it was joining the growing convergence to extend the e-commerce moratorium, with many welcoming the additional progress in narrowing gaps in the discussions.
articles.titleJa: 一般理事会議長、MC14交渉の勢いを基礎とする次のステップを概説
articles.summary: WTO一般理事会議長がMC14後の交渉進展について報告し、電子商取引モラトリアム延長への支持拡大に言及した。
